sections.paragraph: quadril. once occurring Psa 78:47 where, in the other member of the sentence, there is בָּרָד hail; the context shews that it must be something destructive to trees. LXX., Vulg., Saad., Abulwalid, translate it frost, which, however, can hardly be supported on etymological grounds. I, therefore, prefer ants. Comp. Arab. نَمَلُ ants, نَمَلَةُ an ant, with the letter ח prefixed; see חֲבַצֶּלֶת , חִדֶּקֶל . See more in Bochart, Hieroz. iii. page 255, ed. Lips. [Professor Lee suggests locusts, comparing with it Arab. حَِابلُ, etc.]
